label: IDMM
hint: Intelligent Decision-Making Mode keeps a chat/terminal session moving through provider faults and decision stalls. Two independent, default-off watches share one engine: Fault watch and Decision watch.

tier.ruleOnlyDesc: No model: scans by rules and keeps the session alive (auto-retry on faults, auto-pick the first safe option on a choice). Never answers open questions.
tier.rulePlusModelDesc: Rules scan; the backup model decides on your behalf and drives the work to completion (escalates to the model when rules can't decide).

watch.faultDesc: Detect provider/network faults and auto-retry (failover queue arrives later).
watch.decisionDesc: Detect in-session questions (option decisions + open questions) and answer so work never hangs.

strategy.title: Decision strategy
strategy.freeformPlaceholder: Extra guidance appended to the backup-model prompt, e.g. 'keep changes minimal; prefer reversible actions'.
strategy.answerOpenQuestionsHint: Let the backup model write a free-form answer to open-ended questions (backup-model mode only).

sessionBackupHint: Leave empty to prefer the global default backup provider (Settings → Intelligent Decision-Making), then fall back to this session's own model; set one here to apply it to this watch only.
backupRequired: Backup-model mode needs a usable model: pick one here, set a global default in Settings → Intelligent Decision-Making, or enable it on a session that already has a model (its model is reused automatically).

settings.title: Intelligent Decision-Making (IDMM)
settings.desc: Defaults for the backup-model mode, used when a watch does not set its own.
settings.defaultSteeringPlaceholder: Default decision policy applied when a watch leaves its free-form policy empty.

activity.title: Decision activity
activity.desc: Recent IDMM interventions across all sessions (most recent first). This is a rolling window, not a full history — old records age out automatically.
activity.empty: No interventions across any session yet.
activity.clearConfirm: Clear all decision activity across every session? This cannot be undone.
